text: {person} has turned the disputed estate into a furniture shop. Every chair comes with a legal opinion and one refuses cash from known liars.

choices.text: Buy the chair that remembers testimony

outcomes.text: It whispers three names and an upholstery complaint.

choices.text: Offer to settle its remaining paperwork

outcomes.text: The chairs vote you temporary executor. The stools abstain.

text: A probate courier has mistaken an old relic route for a delivery address. Their form cites this history: {record}

choices.text: Correct the route before the relic vanishes

success.text: The courier redraws the route and pays you in legal gratitude, which is apparently gold.

fail.text: The relic is delivered to a cave with excellent standing.

choices.text: Sign as the cave

outcomes.text: The signature is accepted. Geology has weak identity controls.

text: A former refugee now apprentices under {person}. Their medicine works, their paperwork does not, and the guild recognizes only one of those skills.

choices.text: Sponsor the apprentice's examination

outcomes.text: The practical exam saves two patients. The written exam injures a clerk.

choices.text: Teach them to weaponize the paperwork

outcomes.text: By lunch they are licensed, tenured, and investigating the exam board.

text: The refugee quarter has become the city's busiest market. Locals complain while standing in line for its bread.

choices.text: Invest in permanent stalls

outcomes.text: Canvas becomes timber. Temporary addresses become family names.

choices.text: Collect the old temporary permit fees

outcomes.text: You discover the permits were printed on edible paper for a reason.

text: Prosperity has attracted a professional thieves' conference. Registration includes a complimentary replacement wallet.

choices.text: Attend undercover

success.text: You recover three purses, including your own future one.

fail.text: Your disguise is stolen while you are wearing it.

choices.text: Sell them better conference badges

outcomes.text: Every badge opens one door. None opens the advertised door.

text: The strongbox boom has made locks cheap and keys speculative. A key broker offers futures in doors not yet built.

choices.text: Buy the boring physical key

outcomes.text: It opens a nearby cupboard full of better keys.

choices.text: Short the door market

success.text: Three doors collapse financially. You are paid not to explain.

fail.text: You now owe rent to a doorway.

text: The false saint's former followers have elected {person} their interim skeptic. They now require proof before breakfast.

choices.text: Help write a doctrine of reasonable doubt

outcomes.text: The first commandment is a footnote asking for sources.

choices.text: Sell authenticated pieces of the old halo

outcomes.text: Each piece is authentic. The halo was mostly pieces.

text: A roadside shrine now honors honest mistakes. Someone has left {god} a confession, two onions, and a forged receipt for both onions.

choices.text: Add a mistake of your own

outcomes.text: The shrine accepts it without improvement.

choices.text: Correct the receipt

outcomes.text: The onions are innocent. The handwriting is not.

text: Under the reformed charter, {person} has opened a cooperative shop where every price includes the argument that produced it.

choices.text: Buy without reopening negotiations

outcomes.text: The staff applaud your restraint and quietly lower the price.

choices.text: Audit the cooperative books

success.text: You find one error and seventeen democratic explanations.

fail.text: The books elect not to balance today.

text: The new guild sends {pair} and {pair_other} to recruit beyond the city. Their {pair_kind} arrangement was not covered by the travel policy.

choices.text: Teach them how to share credit

outcomes.text: They split the credit unevenly but admit it in writing.

choices.text: Make it a recruitment contest

outcomes.text: They recruit everyone, including each other's witnesses.

text: Mail from the walking town arrives before the town does. The postmark lists a latitude, a mood, and 'probably Tuesday.'

choices.text: Carry the mail toward its predicted route

outcomes.text: You meet the town halfway. It claims this counts as municipal efficiency.

choices.text: Open the letter addressed to the road

outcomes.text: The road is invited to a bridge opening and has nothing suitable to wear.

text: A vendor sells souvenirs from neighborhoods that have not arrived yet. The snow globe contains tomorrow's weather and a tiny tax collector chasing it.

choices.text: Buy tomorrow's weather

outcomes.text: Tomorrow is damp, embarrassed, and nonrefundable.

choices.text: Sell the vendor your current address

outcomes.text: They pay well. By evening, your address has moved without you.

text: {person}, once named by your failed verdict, returns with witnesses, documents, and the calm of someone who has rehearsed this meeting for days.

choices.text: Hear the appeal in full

outcomes.text: The record bends back toward truth. It does not erase the cell door.

choices.text: Insist the old verdict was close enough

outcomes.text: Close enough becomes the first line of their campaign against you.
